name: Zaza

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Zaza (zaza1246).

notes: Skeleton. Zaza (Zazaki), a Northwestern Iranian (Indo-European) language of eastern Anatolia written in a Bedirxan/Hawar-style Latin alphabet. Kurdish-Latin letter values apply: c=[dʒ], ç=[tʃ], ş=[ʃ], j=[ʒ], x=[x], ğ=[ɣ], q=[q]; circumflex vowels ê/î/û are the tense/long counterparts of e/i/u and dotless ı is the central [ɨ]. Stress is not marked, and the pharyngeals [ħ ʕ] of some varieties are not consistently written; both are left unencoded.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Zaza (zaza1246)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
